title: teucrium divaricatum subsp. graecum
intro: Teucrium divaricatum subsp. graecum is a subspecies of the perennial herb Teucrium divaricatum, also known as the Greek germander. It is a plant traditionally used in herbal medicine, with medicinal properties attributed to its essential oils, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Teucrium divaricatum subsp. graecum include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various conditions such as digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Teucrium divaricatum subsp. graecum include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used internally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
